body.title: Define proof-of-work requirements for each cognition migration step
body.result: closed — pe_chain coding mechanism decommissioned; ticket no longer relevant to reasoning-first direction
body.description:
Tooling for validating cognitive pipeline changes is currently positioned as rescue (debug when broken) rather than scheduled validation (prove before claiming done). For the planned graph-tree migration there will be multiple difficult transition passes like the NE splitup — and graph traversal failures produce plausible-looking wrong output, not crashes.

Define what 'proved to work' means at each cognition migration step, including:
1. Behavioral invariant tests — tests that verify cognitive invariants ('stuck for 3 cycles → self-diagnostic subgoal fires'), not just 'NE ran without exception'
2. Required trace reads — which cognitive traces (NE psych log, TWM observations, pe_chain basket) must be reviewed as part of claiming a sprint passed
3. Replay artifact format — what session state snapshot is needed to replay a scenario post-fix
4. Per-step checklist — for each planned graph-tree migration step (NE → graph, dreaming → graph, etc), what is the minimum validation set

This spec becomes the gate for each graph-tree migration sprint: you don't declare it done until the spec's validation set passes.

**Affected files:**
- lab/docs/cognition_migration_validation_spec.md (new)
- Possibly update sprint-ticket skill to require validation checklist for cognition-tagged tickets

**Design rules:** none apply

**Scope boundary:** Spec document + process definition only. Does not implement any new test infrastructure — that follows from the spec.

**Test plan:** no tests — this is a spec/doc ticket; completeness check: does the spec answer 'what must pass before we claim this migration step is done?' for at least 3 planned migration steps
